facts.statement: The mitochondrion is a membrane-bound organelle found in most eukaryotic cells.
questions: What is a mitochondrion?
Where are mitochondria found?
What type of organelle is the mitochondrion?

facts.statement: Mitochondria generate most of the cell's supply of ATP.
questions: What do mitochondria produce?
Why are mitochondria called the powerhouse of the cell?
What is the main function of mitochondria?

facts.statement: Mitochondria have an outer membrane and a folded inner membrane.
questions: What is the structure of mitochondria?
How many membranes do mitochondria have?
What membranes are found in mitochondria?

facts.statement: The folds of the mitochondrial inner membrane are called cristae.
questions: What are cristae?
What are the folds in mitochondria called?
Where are cristae located?

facts.statement: Mitochondria contain their own circular DNA.
questions: Do mitochondria have their own DNA?
What type of DNA do mitochondria contain?
What is unique about mitochondrial genetic material?

facts.statement: Mitochondrial DNA is inherited almost exclusively from the mother.
questions: How is mitochondrial DNA inherited?
Is mitochondrial DNA inherited from the father or mother?
What is the inheritance pattern of mitochondrial DNA?

facts.statement: The endosymbiotic theory proposes that mitochondria descend from free-living bacteria engulfed by an ancestral eukaryotic cell.
questions: What does the endosymbiotic theory propose?
How did mitochondria originate according to endosymbiotic theory?
What is the evolutionary origin of mitochondria?

facts.statement: Lynn Margulis advanced the endosymbiotic theory.
questions: Who advanced the endosymbiotic theory?
What did Lynn Margulis contribute to cell biology?
Who is credited with the endosymbiotic theory?
